seo_description: Current time in Cleveland, Ohio. Eastern Time Zone — EST (UTC-5) in winter, EDT (UTC-4) during DST. Live clock and time differences for Cleveland, OH.

faq.question: What time is it in Cleveland right now?
faq.answer: Cleveland, Ohio, USA uses Eastern Standard Time (EST, UTC-5) in winter and Eastern Daylight Time (EDT, UTC-4) during Daylight Saving Time. The IANA identifier is America/New_York. Cleveland is on the same time as New York, Washington D.C., Miami, and Detroit. The live clock above shows the current Cleveland time.

faq.question: What time zone is Cleveland in?
faq.answer: Cleveland, Ohio is in the Eastern Time Zone (ET). It uses Eastern Standard Time (EST, UTC-5) from the first Sunday in November to the second Sunday in March, and Eastern Daylight Time (EDT, UTC-4) during Daylight Saving Time. The IANA identifier is America/New_York. Cleveland shares its time zone with Columbus, Cincinnati, New York, and Detroit.

faq.question: Does Cleveland observe daylight saving time?
faq.answer: Yes. Cleveland, Ohio observes Daylight Saving Time. Clocks spring forward 1 hour on the second Sunday in March at 2:00 AM (EST → EDT) and fall back on the first Sunday in November. Ohio follows the standard US DST schedule.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Cleveland and Chicago?
faq.answer: Cleveland (EST/EDT, Eastern Time) is always 1 hour ahead of Chicago (CST/CDT, Central Time). Both cities observe DST on the same schedule, keeping the gap constant year-round. When it is 9:00 AM in Chicago, it is 10:00 AM in Cleveland.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Cleveland and New York?
faq.answer: Cleveland (EST/EDT) and New York (EST/EDT) are in the same Eastern Time Zone. There is no time difference between them — both use the same UTC offset year-round. When it is noon in New York, it is noon in Cleveland.

faq.question: What are typical business hours in Cleveland?
faq.answer: Standard business hours in Cleveland are 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM ET, Monday through Friday. Cleveland is Ohio's second-largest city and a major healthcare and manufacturing hub. Major employers include Cleveland Clinic (one of the world's top hospitals), University Hospitals, KeyBank, and Progressive Corporation. The Cleveland port on Lake Erie supports Great Lakes shipping. The Flats and downtown entertainment districts operate evenings and weekends.
